mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in nominal retail sales signals improving household demand and near-term revenue momentum, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as earnings expectations are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 397666 as of the 2011-11-15 decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce positive 20-day return Rank IC above 0.03 in walk-forward tests or if top-quintile RSAFS growth has lower mean returns than bottom-quintile growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above a high-stress threshold, labor income risk dominates valuation support and investors mark down cyclical earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 9.0 as of the 2011-11-15 decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, a very low federal funds rate combined with non-accelerating CPI reduces discount-rate pressure without forcing immediate tightening, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation rate does not rise versus the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.07 as of the 2011-11-15 decision snapshot
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary easing regime
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-rate and non-accelerating-inflation condition has 60-day forward returns below the unconditional mean in walk-forward testing or a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A downside break in industrial production indicates weakening factory output and operating leverage, leading analysts to cut earnings estimates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 94.1851 as of the 2011-11-15 decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered INDPRO downside breaks are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not lower than non-triggered returns.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts from depressed levels signals improving construction demand, credit availability, and household balance-sheet repair, causing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST below 800 and rises more than 5% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 658 as of the 2011-11-15 decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebound triggers from below 800 do not deliver higher 60-day mean S&P 500 returns than non-triggered periods or if the hit rate is below 50% after transaction-cost-neutral classification.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll expansion paired with a falling unemployment rate confirms broad labor-market healing rather than labor-force dropout, raising expected consumption and earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change above 0 and UNRATE 3-month change below 0
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 131516 as of the 2011-11-15 decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-up unemployment-down condition fails to outperform the unconditional 20-day return mean or if either leg alone explains all predictive power in interaction tests.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI growth exceeds nominal retail-sales growth, inflation erodes real consumption momentum and raises margin and policy-tightening risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month percent change exceeds RSAFS 3-month percent change
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 226.955 as of the 2011-11-15 decision snapshot
regime_hint: inflation squeeze with weak real demand
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-squeeze observations have non-negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the interaction term between CPIAUCSL growth and RSAFS growth is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A downside crossing in real GDP growth below stall speed signals recession-risk repricing and broad earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 13352.8 as of the 2011-11-15 decision snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 stall-speed triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered downside returns are not statistically below baseline at p < 0.10.
